steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question asking what both authors would most likely agree with. Both texts discuss collaboration between tribal groups and government agencies in conservation work. The point of agreement must be supported by both passages, not just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘A collaborative approach to conservation is likely to help reduce the predicted temperature increases in the Great Lakes region.’: Neither text claims that collaboration reduces temperatures — both discuss collaboration as a way to improve conservation outcomes, not to control climate. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In the Great Lakes region, conservation efforts focused on the northern wild rice plant are likely to be more effective than those focused on the American crow.’: This compares the two species' conservation prospects, but neither author makes such a comparative claim about effectiveness of efforts. Text 1 only notes that wild rice will fare worse than the crow under rising temperatures. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Natural resources conservation projects are likely to benefit from collaboration between tribal and government groups.’: Text 1 states the collaborative approach "will likely improve the quality of any joint conservation projects," and Text 2 states it "tends to lead to better conservation outcomes." Both authors directly support this claim. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘In the Great Lakes region, it is more important to focus conservation efforts on the northern wild rice plant than it is on the American crow.’: Neither author ranks species by conservation priority in this way; Text 1 discusses relative climate vulnerability, not relative importance of conservation focus. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
